pi_items.verified_value: Sector_Rotation is a valid instrument list name
pi_items.source: NinjaTrader 8 allows arbitrary custom list names; verified against platform documentation

pi_items.verified_value: All three are valid, real, supported stock symbols in NinjaTrader 8: SPY (S&P 500 ETF), AAPL (Apple Inc), MSFT (Microsoft Corp)
pi_items.source: Official NinjaTrader documentation and Yahoo Finance - confirmed as active trading symbols on NASDAQ/major exchanges

pi_items.verified_value: Instrument Lists are stored in (My) Documents > NinjaTrader 8 > db, in NinjaTrader.sqlite database file, NOT in the templates folder
pi_items.source: Official NinjaTrader support forum (https://forum.ninjatrader.com) and operations documentation - Instrument Lists are database entries, not template files

pi_items.verified_value: Path structure is plausible; matches pattern used in other NinjaTrader tasks in this environment (setup_ninjatrader.ps1 creates this directory)
pi_items.source: Verified against setup_ninjatrader.ps1 script which creates C:\Users\Docker\Desktop\NinjaTraderTasks directory at lines 19-20

pi_items.metadata_value: real Yahoo Finance data (SPY, AAPL, MSFT 2023-2024)
pi_items.verified_value: Confirmed: Data files SPY.Last.txt, AAPL.Last.txt, MSFT.Last.txt contain OHLCV stock data ranging from 2023-01-03 (20230103) to 2024-12-31 (20241231), 502 rows each
pi_items.source: Direct file inspection of /examples/ninja_trader_env/data/ - OHLCV format with historical pricing

privileged_info_summary: The task uses real stock market data for three actual NASDAQ/ETF-traded instruments (SPY, AAPL, MSFT) covering the period January 2023 through December 2024. The expected instruments are valid stock symbols supported by NinjaTrader 8. The list name 'Sector_Rotation' is a valid custom name. However, the metadata claims Instrument Lists are stored in C:\Users\Docker\Documents\NinjaTrader 8\templates\InstrumentLists, which contradicts official NinjaTrader documentation stating they are actually stored in the NinjaTrader.sqlite database file in the db folder.
